channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, US
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, US.

item.title: Prairie Ronde Artist Residency: Summer/Fall 2026 (Michigan)
item.description:
Organisation: The Mill at Vicksburg

Location: Vicksburg, Michigan, USA (5-6 week on-site residency, private housing)

Award: $2,000 stipend plus a $500 travel grant and private housing for the 5-6 week residency. Application fee: $25.

Duration: Five- to six-week residency

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026

Prairie Ronde Artist Residency at The Mill at Vicksburg, a self-directed residency open to artists across disciplines including film, video and new media. ELIGIBILITY: artists of any discipline; open worldwide. Apply via the Prairie Ronde application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Trust: Grants for Artist Projects (GAP) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Artist Trust

Location: Washington State, United States

Award: $2,500 unrestricted project grant per artist (awarded to 65 artists). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

Unrestricted project-based grants of $2,500 from Artist Trust to 65 Washington State artists across all disciplines, including literary and media arts. Funds can support a specific project or career-advancing activity. ELIGIBILITY: Washington State residents who are originators of works of art and not current students; open to artist teams.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lanesboro Arts: Early Career Artist Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Lanesboro Arts

Location: Lanesboro, Minnesota, United States (pop. 754)

Award: $1,250 weekly stipend (fixed per residency, including for small collaborating teams); housing provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two or four week residency, scheduled within February 1 to December 31, 2027

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Residency program in the rural community of Lanesboro, MN, supported by the Jerome Foundation, offering early career artists two or four week residencies to explore how their work can engage the community and how a rural community can inform their work. Staff co-create a fully customized residency experience around community engagement. Lanesboro Arts offers two to three residencies per year; the program provides an individual studio plus access to conference rooms, desk space, a gallery, piano and stage, with private housing (apartment, kitchen, private room and bathroom). No meals provided. Open to all disciplines. ELIGIBILITY: legal residents of Minnesota or one of the five boroughs of New York City for at least one year prior to applying; defined as early career artists with at least a 2-year and no more than 10-year track record of creating and presenting their own projects (not first-time creators or beginning artists). Degree-seeking students at the time of application or during the grant period (K-12, college, graduate or postgraduate) are not eligible. Age is not a factor. The primary goal must be to generate new work rather than remount or re-interpret existing work. Apply via the online webform on the Lanesboro Arts website. Selection announced June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence 2026 (Oak Spring)
item.description:
Organisation: Oak Spring Garden Foundation

Location: Upperville, Virginia, USA (2-5 week on-site residency)

Award: $10,000 grant plus a two- to five-week residency with accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two- to five-week residency

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence from the Oak Spring Garden Foundation, supporting early-career artists whose work engages plants, gardens and landscapes. ELIGIBILITY: early-career visual and literary artists, dancers and musicians; no nationality restriction stated, though the residency takes place on site in Virginia. Apply via the Oak Spring fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Light Work Artist-in-Residence 2027 (Syracuse, NY)
item.description:
Organisation: Light Work

Location: Syracuse, New York, USA (in-person, one-month residency on site)

Award: $7,500 stipend plus a furnished apartment and access to state-of-the-art photography facilities. The residency concludes with a feature in a special edition of 'Contact Sheet: The Light Work Annual' alongside a commissioned essay. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency in 2027

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Light Work, an independent non-profit founded in 1973, offers a long-running artist-in-residence programme dedicated to the development of experimental and contemporary photographic practices. Over 400 artists have participated in the residency, with many going on to gain international recognition. ELIGIBILITY: artists working in photography or image-based media, from any country and at any career stage. Apply via the Light Work SlideRoom portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco)
item.description:
Organisation: The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Headlands Center for the Arts: Artist in Residence 2027 (AIR)
item.description:
Organisation: Headlands Center for the Arts

Location: Sausalito, California, United States (Marin Headlands, Golden Gate National Recreation Area)

Award: Fully sponsored residency: studio space, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and living-expense stipend. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 10 weeks, scheduled between January and December 2027

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Open call for the 2027 cohort of the Headlands Artist in Residence (AIR) program, one of the longer-running fully-sponsored residencies on the US West Coast. Approximately 50 local, national and international artists are selected each year for a residency of 4 to 10 weeks at the Headlands' Marin Headlands campus (former military buildings inside the Golden Gate National Recreation Area, just north of San Francisco). Residents receive a private studio, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and a living-expense stipend, fully sponsored by the program. Open to artists at all career stages working in any medium: drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, film, video, new media, installation, fiction and nonfiction writing, poetry, dance, music, interdisciplinary practice, social practice, and architecture. Strong fit for new-media / interdisciplinary / research-driven practices. Application opens 1 April 2026 and closes 1 June 2026 via SlideRoom; applicants are notified of decisions by 11 December 2026. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Third Wave Fund: Artist-in-Residence Program 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Third Wave Fund

Location: Remote (must be currently residing in the United States or US Territories; valid SSN or EIN required)

Award: $10,000 stipend

Duration: 5 months (13 July 2026 to 10 December 2026)

Deadline: Closed 13 May 2026

Annual paid virtual residency for one emerging visual artist with skills in digital illustration and light animation, supporting Third Wave Fund's gender-justice and liberation visibility, fundraising and donor mobilisation. Deliverables in 2026: two digital illustrations and one short-form animated reel for storytelling and fundraising. Five-month residency with a $10,000 stipend, mentorship and guidance from long-time movement artists, feedback sessions, experience and portfolio building, and social-media spotlight opportunities. Eligibility: 18+; valid SSN or EIN; currently residing in the US or US Territories; emerging visual artist (early career, generally up to 10 years of practice; no degree-granting art-school enrolment); skilled in digital illustration and light animation; available for the full schedule. Schedule: orientation 14 July 2026; project meetings 21 July, 16 September, 4 November; mentoring session 14 October; debrief 10 December 2026. TWF is a fully remote organisation operating a 4-day work week (Monday to Thursday); all communications and meetings happen Mon-Thu via Zoom and email. Deadline 13 May 2026 at 17:00 CST. Notifications go out in the first week of June 2026. Questions: comms@thirdwavefund.org (response within ~3 Mon-Thu business days).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Bennett Prize 2027 (Round 5)
item.description:
Organisation: The Bennett Prize / Muskegon Museum of Art

Location: Muskegon, Michigan, US (national reach)

Award: $75,000 ($37,500/year over 2 years) + traveling solo exhibition; additional $10,000 for one finalist

Duration: 2 year award period

Deadline: 19 Sep 2026

$75,000 prize for women figurative realist painters, awarded by a five-member jury. The winner receives $37,500 each year for two years to create a solo exhibition that travels nationally; one finalist additionally receives $10,000. Open to emerging artists who have not yet achieved full professional recognition.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CultureHub Residency Program 2026 (LA + NY tracks)
item.description:
Organisation: CultureHub (with international partners DOCKdigital Berlin, Société des arts technologiques Montréal, La MaMa Umbria Spoleto)

Location: Los Angeles, CA OR New York, NY (NY track includes international leg in Berlin / Montréal / Spoleto)

Award: $2,000 stipend (both tracks). NY track additionally covers international travel and housing for the partner-location week.

Duration: LA: 1 to 2 weeks at the LA studio. NY: 1 week at the NYC studio + 1 week at one of three international partner locations.

Deadline: Closed 20 May 2026

Two-track residency program for artists experimenting with emerging technologies (telepresence, VR/AR, AI, robotics, creative coding, live web, game design, etc.) in search of new artistic forms. One application form; pick one track. LA TRACK: 3 residents get 1 to 2 weeks at the LA studio (5.1 Kalio audio surround, modular configuration, flexible exhibition space). LA proposals favoured: immersive audio environments, installations, experimental spatial design, tech-infused textiles/sculpture, sensory/physical exploration. Themes of particular interest: ecological responsibility, tech ethics, integration of old and new tech, localised networks, human connection, sustainability. Applicants must be based in LA. NY TRACK: 3 residents (one per international partner) get 1 week at the NYC studio + 1 week at the international partner with a thematic focus: Space + Algorithms at DOCKdigital (Berlin); Hybridization of Spaces & Telematic Installation at Société des arts technologiques (Montréal); Data, Narrative, and Performance at La MaMa Umbria (Spoleto). Applicants must be based in NY. Both tracks: $2,000 stipend, public offering during residency (work-in-progress showing, talk, workshop, etc.), marketing and documentation support, access to the residency cohort and network of past residents. Application closes 20 May 2026, 11:59 PM PT.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Backslash Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Backslash (Cornell Tech)

Location: Cornell Tech, New York City, US

Award: $50,000 ($20K artist stipend + $20K materials + $10K collaborator) + studio space

Duration: 1 year Visiting Fellow appointment (academic year, starts September)

Deadline: Closed 08 May 2026

$50,000 fellowship supporting an established practicing artist to create a significant new work engaging bleeding-edge digital research and technology, with sustained teamwork from a dedicated technical PhD or faculty collaborator at Cornell Tech. Fellow receives a one-year Visiting Fellow appointment (individual, not studio-contractual), $20,000 artist stipend, $20,000 project materials budget, $10,000 to support the collaborator, on-campus studio space, and badged campus access. 'Established' means a sustained professional practice with institutional recognition (major exhibitions, significant grants/fellowships, published critical coverage, distinct developed practice). Three-stage selection: written project proposal (2 to 4 pages) reviewed by Backslash Team and potential collaborators, then an interview with the candidate collaborator and a Backslash Team member to confirm technical fit, then a single Fellow is selected. Research areas open for collaboration include computational/digital fabrication and accessibility, public interaction with autonomous systems and robotics/HRI, computer vision and graphics (controllable generation, 2D/3D), AI algorithmic fairness and statistics, urban planning + AI fairness, and AI interpretability and human-AI interfaces (with named labs and faculty listed on the call). Fellow responsibilities: collaborate deeply with the assigned collaborator, show the resulting work (Cornell Tech and via the artist's own channels), contribute culturally to campus via a public-facing event and an intimate mentorship/dialogue session, and document the work. Backslash explicitly favours nonlinear, unconventional and exceptional approaches to emerging tech.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MTA Arts & Design Digital Art Program 2026 Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: MTA Arts & Design

Location: New York City, USA (Fulton Transit Center, Grand Central Madison, Moynihan Train Hall)

Award: $1,000 honorarium for finalist proposals + project budget for selected artist(s) (TBD by project nature)

Duration: Up to 2-minute works on display ~3 months in 2027

Deadline: Closed 28 May 2026

Open call for digital and new-media artists to submit existing works for consideration on transit-center screens (video, film, animation, augmented reality, net art, game engines, generative art). Multi-channel installation experience encouraged. Finalists develop site-specific proposals with a $1,000 honorarium; selected artists oversee production and installation with project management from MTA Arts & Design. Sites have no audio. Valid U.S. Taxpayer ID required. Submit up to 5 works (.mov/.mp4) and 10 stills (.jpg/.png) plus bio, CV and artist statement. Deadline 23:59 EST Thursday 28 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: LACMA Art + Technology Lab Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Los Angeles County Museum of Art (LACMA), presented by Hyundai Motor with Snap Inc. and Anthropic

Location: Worldwide (recipients need not be in Los Angeles)

Award: Up to $50,000 per project + in-kind mentorship and tech access (Hyundai, Snap, Anthropic, MIT Media Lab Space Exploration Initiative, NASA JPL)

Duration: ~24 months (2-year cycles)

Deadline: Closed 22 Apr 2026

Rapid-prototyping grant for 3-5 artist-led projects (plus up to 2 invitational) exploring emerging technology and culture. Safe-to-fail environment, finished work deliberately deemphasized. Prior tech experience not required. Notifications by email in July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MacDowell Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: MacDowell

Location: Peterborough, New Hampshire, USA

Award: No residency fee; need-based stipends and travel reimbursement available; ~300 fellowships/year

Duration: Up to 8 weeks (Spring/Summer 2027 cycle: 1 March to 31 August 2027)

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

Residency for artists across seven disciplines (architecture, film/video, interdisciplinary, literature, music composition, theatre, visual arts). Sole selection criterion is artistic excellence. Applications open 15 August 2026. February deadline of the following year covers the Fall/Winter cycle.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
